perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark as part of its company name and in connection with the operation of its business, and the Licensor is willing to grant the Licensee a license to use the Licensed Mark, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark as part of its company name and in connection with the operation of its business, and the Licensor is open to consider granting the Licensee a license to use the Licensed Mark, subject to further negotiation and the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Licensor 'is willing' to grant the license. The modified text states the Licensor is 'open to consider granting', creating uncertainty and contradiction. The original text implies agreement, whereas the modified text suggests willingness to negotiate, making the initial grant of license uncertain and introduces ambiguity about the obligations within the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 1 LICENSE GRANT 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, neither the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed sixty (60) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.justification: The original text indicated a willingness to grant a license, establishing a foundation for the agreement, as seen in 'WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark...and the Licensor is willing to grant the Licensee a license...' The changed text modifies this to 'WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark...and the Licensor is open to consider granting...' This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the later 'ARTICLE 1 LICENSE GRANT 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee...', which assumes the license is already granted. This falls under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition, as key obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor may consider granting to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby expresses interest in accepting from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with some of the Licensed Services and certain business aspects provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: Original text firmly 'grants' a license. The modified text introduces the conditional 'may consider granting' from the Licensor and 'expresses interest' by the Licensee implying the grant isn't definite. Also replaces 'the Licensed Services and any business' with 'some of the Licensed Services and certain business aspects' This creates confusion regarding the scope of the license and directly contradicts the specific services outlined in the 'WHEREAS' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS, the Licensee is a closed-end management investment company that intends to elect to be treated as a business development company under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended; WHEREAS, the Licensor, together with its affiliates, provides investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services; WHEREAS, the Licensor, of which New Mountain Finance Advisers, BDC, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the "Investment Advisor") is an affiliate, is the owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the mark "NMF" (the "Licensed Mark") in the United States of America, Canada and the European Union (the "Territory") in connection with "financial services, namely, investment advisory and investment management services for pooled investment vehicles, private investment funds, and investment accounts; investment management services for others; private equity services, namely, providing expansion and growth capital in the form of private equity investments; financial services, namely, private equity and public equity capital investment; private and public equity investment management services; providing private equity fund investments; investment services, namely, asset acquisition, consultation, development, research and management services; capital investment services; equity capital investment; financial services, namely, debt and equity investment services in private companies, namely, investment to support business expansions, acquisitions, management buyouts and recapitalizations" (the "Licensed Services"), and Licensor has been and is currently using, either on its own or through its related companies or licensees (such as, but not limited to, the Investment Advisor) the Licensed Mark; WHEREAS, the Licensee is entering into an investment advisory and management agreement with the Investment Advisor (the "Investment Management Agreement"), wherein the Licensee will engage the Investment Advisor to act as the investment advisor to the Licensee; WHEREAS, it is intended that the Investment Advisor be a third party beneficiary of this Agreement; and WHEREAS, the Licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark as part of its company name and in connection with the operation of its business, and the Licensor is willing to grant the Licensee a license to use the Licensed Mark, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants a license, while the changed text suggests the licensor 'may consider granting' and the licensee 'expresses interest'. This introduces uncertainty about whether the license is actually granted. Also, the change from 'the Licensed Services and any business' to 'some of the Licensed Services and certain business aspects' narrows the scope of the license. This contradicts the 'WHEREAS' section, which specifies the 'Licensed Services' as a defined set of financial services and indicates the licensee desires to use the Licensed Mark 'in connection with the operation of its business', implying all aspects of the business. This change creates a clear conflict, as the original agreement covered all licensed services, while the modification limits it to some, creating ambiguity and a direct contradiction. The 'may consider granting' and 'expresses interest' language further creates an inconsistency regarding the obligations between Licensor and Licensee with respect to this Agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, all rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement with respect to the Licensed Mark shall cease, and the Licensee shall immediately delete the term "NMF" from its corporate name and shall discontinue all other use of the Licensed Mark.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon expiration or possible termination of this Agreement, whether or not all rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement with respect to the Licensed Mark have been fully established, the Licensee should consider ceasing use of NMF, but immediate deletion of the term "NMF" from its corporate name is subject to further discussion and the discontinuance of all other use of the Licensed Mark remains optional.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the cessation of rights and immediate deletion of 'NMF' upon termination. The revised text introduces uncertainty by stating 'possible termination,' questioning whether rights have been 'fully established,' making the deletion of 'NMF' 'subject to further discussion,' and making the 'discontinuance of all other use of the Licensed Mark remains optional.' This undermines the enforceability of the termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 1 LICENSE GRANT 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, neither the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed sixty (60) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.justification: The original text unequivocally mandates the cessation of rights and immediate deletion of 'NMF' upon termination. In contrast, the changed text introduces ambiguity by suggesting a 'possible termination,' questioning the full establishment of rights, making the deletion of 'NMF' open to 'further discussion,' and rendering the discontinuance of the Licensed Mark 'optional.' This directly contradicts Article 1, which grants a license to use the Licensed Mark under specific terms and conditions, implying a degree of control and enforceability that the changed text undermines. The original text implies the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under the agreement, but the Changed text makes discontinuance of use optional, contradicting the terms of Article 1.
